wait_events.db file sequential read: High sequential I/O latency indicates possible index inefficiency or random reads. Review storage latency and index usage.
wait_events.enq: TX - row lock contention: High row-lock waits suggest contention on DML-heavy tables. Tune commit frequency or review application locking logic.
wait_events.log file sync: Frequent commits or slow redo log I/O. Increase redo log size or separate redo onto low-latency storage.

sql.high_elapsed_time: SQL consuming significant elapsed time; optimize joins and filter predicates.
sql.high_cpu_time: CPU-intensive SQL. Review execution plan, indexes, and statistics.
sql.high_io_wait: High User I/O time per execution. Investigate large scans or missing indexes.

segments.logical_reads: Hot buffer cache usage. Consider partitioning or caching strategies.
segments.physical_reads: High physical reads suggest inefficient I/O path; validate segment placement and indexing.
segments.buffer_busy_waits: Buffer contention detected. Review concurrency on hot blocks and implement partitioning if possible.

general.note: These rules are applied dynamically in the correlation drawer when a row or pie slice is selected.
